name: Yoga the Iyengar Way — Course I
subtitle: Foundation
schemeOfPractice: Alternate lessons 1 and 2 in the first week, then lessons 2 and 3 in the second week, and so on until by week 12 all lessons have been done twice. Finally, consolidate practice by repeating all the lessons once more.
cautions: Take particular care of the knees. Learn to straighten and extend them without straining.

lessons.label: Lesson 1
instructorNotes: Do Uttānāsana I between standing poses or after 2 poses, to rest. If back aches, place hands on ledge. Repeat standing poses twice each.
Learn to adjust height of blankets in Sarvāṅgāsana.
If Sarvāṅgāsana is difficult, lie with legs up against a wall (p. 80).

lessons.label: Lesson 2

poses.displayName: Utthita Hasta Pādāṅguṣṭhāsana I and II

instructorNotes: Rest in Uttānāsana I after Vīrabhadrāsana I.
Before doing Sarvāṅgāsana, lie down and stretch.
In Śavāsana, learn to lie straight.

lessons.label: Lesson 3

poses.displayName: Utthita Hasta Pādāṅguṣṭhāsana I and II

instructorNotes: Keep arms up in Vīrabhadrāsana I, Trikoṇāsana, Pārśvakoṇāsana, Vīrabhadrāsana II, and Pārśvottānāsana — these are the five basic standing poses.

lessons.label: Lesson 5
instructorNotes: In sitting poses, concentrate on turning hips.

lessons.label: Lesson 7
instructorNotes: Observe breathing in postures. In forward bends, synchronize actions with breath.

lessons.label: Lesson 9
instructorNotes: Stay in Vīrāsana and Padmāsana to establish tranquility in forward bends that follow.

name: Yoga the Iyengar Way — Course II
subtitle: General Practice

lessons.label: Lesson 1B (Sitting)
instructorNotes: Stay in Vīrāsana and Padmāsana to establish tranquility in forward bends that follow.

lessons.label: Lesson 1C (Jumpings and Balancings)
instructorNotes: In Adho Mukha Vṛkṣāsana, practice kicking up until you can kick the knees. Learn to drop back from Uttānāsana into Setu Bandha Sarvāṅgāsana and jump up again.

poses.displayName: Viloma Prāṇāyāma I and II, lying down

lessons.label: Lesson 2A (Standing)
instructorNotes: Repeat reverse standing poses several times each.

lessons.label: Lesson 2B (Sitting)
instructorNotes: Observe breathing in postures. In forward bends, synchronize actions with breath.

lessons.label: Lesson 2C (Backbends)
instructorNotes: Press sacrum down to keeping body centered between arms and legs. In twists, do intermediate stage.

lessons.label: Lesson 2D (Relaxation and Pranayama)

poses.displayName: Viloma Prāṇāyāma I and II, lying down (pp. 161–2)

instructorNotes: Make inhalations and exhalations same length and volume.

poses.displayName: Ūrdhva Mukha Śvānāsana without ledge Uttānāsana

lessons.label: Lesson 3B (Sitting)
instructorNotes: In twists, concentrate on turning hips.

lessons.label: Lesson 3C (Inverted)
instructorNotes: In twists, concentrate on turning hips.
Learn to drop back with one leg into Eka Pāda Sīrṣāsana from Sarvāṅgāsana.

poses.displayName: Ujjāyi Prāṇāyāma I and II, lying down
Viloma Prāṇāyāma I and II, lying down (pp. 161–2)
Ūrdhva Mukha Śvānāsana without ledge Uttānāsana

lessons.label: Lesson 4C (Backbends)

poses.displayName: Dvi Pāda Viparīta Daṇḍāsana from Sīrṣāsana

instructorNotes: Do Ūrdhva Dhanurasana several times in different ways. In Ūrdhva Dhanurasana, drop back to wall and aim gradually to release. Go back to rest, come back to front legs and do not force knees back to release gradually, extending it.

lessons.label: Lesson 4D (Pranayama)
instructorNotes: Quality, not quantity, of normal breathing sitting (p. 153).
Be careful not to overstrain.

name: Yoga the Iyengar Way — Course III
subtitle: Intermediate
schemeOfPractice: Practice may be arranged so that each day of the week has a different emphasis, with, for example, standing poses on one day, sitting poses the next, then backbends. Other postures may also be included. Practiced Prāṇāyāma may be done at a different time of day or separately, at a different time of day.
cautions: It is essential first to learn pranayama under the guidance of a teacher.

poses.displayName: Ūrdhva Dhanurasana × 3; Dvi Pāda Viparīta Daṇḍāsana
Adho Mukha Śvānāsana × 3; Ūrdhva Dhanurasana from Sīrṣāsana
Ūrdhva Dhanurasana × 6; Dvi Pāda Viparīta Daṇḍāsana
3× Adho Mukha Śvānāsana; then lift up into Ūrdhva Dhanurasana from Uttānāsana

lessons.label: Lesson 6 (Backbends)
instructorNotes: CONSOLIDATION: Repeat all the poses and aim for steadiness in the intermediate stages. Absorb the purpose of the intermediate stages. Understand the backbends. Be careful not to overexert. Quality not quantity of normal breathing sitting (p. 153). Pranayama is important.

name: Yoga the Iyengar Way — Course IV
subtitle: Advanced
schemeOfPractice: Practice may be arranged so that each day of the week has a different emphasis with, for example, standing poses on one day, sitting, twists, backbends. Other postures may also be included. Practiced Prāṇāyāma may be done at a different time of day.
cautions: Do not go on to Course IV until you have achieved a basis to work more elaborately on backbends.

poses.displayName: Viparīta Daṇḍāsana on chair (p. 136) × 3; Dvi Pāda Viparīta Daṇḍāsana
Adho Mukha Vṛkṣāsana × 3; Ūrdhva Dhanurasana from Sīrṣāsana
Ūrdhva Dhanurasana × 6; Dvi Pāda Viparīta Daṇḍāsana
3× Adho Mukha Śvānāsana; then lift up into Ūrdhva Dhanurasana from Uttānāsana
